subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Shellfish Toxins
description: A short lesson to help you apply Shellfish Toxins.

hook.text: Red tide doesn't just kill fish. Shellfish that filter the toxic algae become poison factories.

buildup.text: Harmful algal blooms produce neurotoxins that accumulate in mussels, clams, and oysters.

discovery.text: Paralytic shellfish poisoning causes tingling, then numbness, then respiratory paralysis.

twist.text: Cooking doesn't destroy the toxin—heat-stable means boiling won't make contaminated shellfish safe.

climax.text: Shellfish harvesting bans during red tide events are the only reliable prevention.

punchline.text: The ocean cooked it for you. The toxin is still there. Trust the warning signs.

quiz.question: Why can't cooking make toxin-contaminated shellfish safe?
options: The toxins are heat-stable and survive cooking
The toxins are in the shell, not the meat
